title: quercus petraea subsp. pinnatiloba
intro: Quercus petraea subsp. pinnatiloba, also known as sessile oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree in the beech family Fagaceae. The leaves of the Quercus petraea subsp. pinnatiloba plant have been found to have astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The medicinal properties of this plant have been used to treat various health issues, including diarrhea, fever, and skin conditions, due to its ability to soothe and protect the body. Herbal preparations of Quercus petraea subsp. pinnatiloba can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its health benefits and medicinal properties.
